title: MITRE ATT&CK for ICS — Technique to Defensive Controls Mapping
description: Maps MITRE ATT&CK for ICS techniques to the controls library entries and IEC 62443 SRs that prevent or detect each technique. Use to prioritise controls based on threat actor TTPs or to understand which controls address specific attack scenarios.
verificationNote: MITRE ATT&CK for ICS technique IDs are public domain from attack.mitre.org/matrices/ics. Control mappings are reasoned assessments — not normatively specified.

mappings.name: Activate Firmware Update Mode
mappings.primaryMitigation: Restrict engineering access via PAM; disable firmware update mode when not in active maintenance; monitor for mode changes via OT monitoring platform.

mappings.name: Monitor Process State
mappings.primaryMitigation: Network segmentation prevents unauthorised access to process data; passive OT monitoring detects anomalous historian or data collection activity.

mappings.name: Automated Collection
mappings.primaryMitigation: Zone segmentation limits scope of automated data collection; OT network monitoring detects mass data collection patterns.

mappings.name: Block Command Message
mappings.primaryMitigation: OT monitoring detects command drop anomalies; redundant control paths maintain availability; backups enable rapid recovery.

mappings.name: Block Reporting Message
mappings.primaryMitigation: OT monitoring with network-level visibility detects silenced reporting; alarm management reviews detect anomalous silence.

mappings.name: Brute Force I/O
mappings.primaryMitigation: Input validation at safety instrumented systems; OT-aware DPI blocks malformed protocol messages; passive monitoring detects I/O anomalies.

mappings.name: Command-Line Interface
mappings.primaryMitigation: Restrict CLI access via RBAC; application whitelisting on OT workstations; PAM session recording captures CLI commands.

mappings.name: Control Device
mappings.primaryMitigation: Individual accounts and RBAC limit who can issue control commands; zone segmentation limits lateral reach; OT monitoring detects anomalous control commands.

mappings.name: Data Destruction
mappings.primaryMitigation: Offline immutable backups survive data destruction; OT monitoring detects mass deletion events; log protection (SR-3.9) preserves incident evidence.

mappings.name: Data from Information Repositories
mappings.primaryMitigation: RBAC on historian restricts bulk data access; access logging detects unusual data exports; IDMZ prevents direct historian access from IT.

mappings.name: Default Credentials
mappings.primaryMitigation: Systematic default credential elimination at commissioning and annual audit. This is the highest-impact, lowest-cost OT control.

mappings.name: Denial of Control
mappings.primaryMitigation: Zone segmentation limits denial-of-control attack surface; redundant control paths; OT monitoring detects control session anomalies.

mappings.name: Denial of Service
mappings.primaryMitigation: Rate limiting at zone boundaries; OT-aware firewall blocks malformed protocol traffic; passive monitoring detects DoS patterns without disrupting production.

mappings.name: Denial of View
mappings.primaryMitigation: Independent OT network monitoring provides alternative view of process state; redundant HMI connectivity; historian data as backup view.

mappings.name: Device Restart/Shutdown
mappings.primaryMitigation: RBAC limits who can issue restart/shutdown commands; OT monitoring detects unexpected device restarts; redundant control hardware for critical systems.

mappings.name: Drive-by Compromise
mappings.primaryMitigation: Block internet access on all OT workstations; application whitelisting prevents execution of malicious downloads; browser isolation for any permitted web access.

mappings.name: Engineering Workstation Compromise
mappings.primaryMitigation: Application whitelisting on EWS; MFA for EWS access; PAM session recording for all engineering sessions; OT monitoring detects anomalous PLC programming activity.

mappings.name: Exploit Public-Facing Application
mappings.primaryMitigation: Minimise OT-facing services (SR-7.7 least functionality); patch all externally reachable services; IDMZ limits exposure of OT services to IT network.

mappings.name: Exploitation of Remote Services
mappings.primaryMitigation: OT remote access gateway as single point of entry with MFA; disable RDP, VNC on OT workstations unless accessed via PAM; patch remote access vulnerabilities promptly.

mappings.name: Modify Controller Tasking
mappings.primaryMitigation: PLC program integrity monitoring; OT monitoring detects anomalous programming sessions; change management requires authorisation for any controller modification.

mappings.name: External Remote Services
mappings.primaryMitigation: Enforce all remote OT access through a single, monitored gateway with MFA. Colonial Pipeline (2021) exploited a VPN without MFA — this control directly prevents that attack pattern.

mappings.name: Loss of Availability
mappings.primaryMitigation: Redundant control systems for critical functions; OT backup enables rapid recovery; OT monitoring provides early warning of degrading availability.

mappings.name: Loss of Control
mappings.primaryMitigation: Safety Instrumented Systems as last line of defence for safety-critical loss of control scenarios; redundant control hardware; defined fail-safe states.

mappings.name: Loss of Productivity and Revenue
mappings.primaryMitigation: Rapid recovery capability reduces operational downtime; incident response plan with operations engineering input; cyber insurance for residual financial risk.

mappings.name: Loss of Safety
mappings.primaryMitigation: Physical SIS isolation (SL 4) is the primary control; independent hardware interlocks; input validation on safety-critical setpoints; two-person rule for SIS work.

mappings.name: Adversary-in-the-Middle
mappings.primaryMitigation: TLS for all inter-zone communications; OT-aware DPI at zone boundaries detects traffic anomalies; passive monitoring detects ARP spoofing and network anomalies.

mappings.name: Manipulation of Control
mappings.primaryMitigation: RBAC limits who can send control commands; input validation on control setpoints; OT monitoring detects anomalous control sequences; hardware interlocks for safety-critical limits.

mappings.name: Manipulation of View
mappings.primaryMitigation: Independent OT network monitoring provides ground truth independent of HMI display; historian integrity controls detect data manipulation; operator alarm management.

mappings.name: Modify Alarm Settings
mappings.primaryMitigation: RBAC restricts who can modify alarm settings; change management for alarm configuration changes; configuration monitoring detects alarm threshold changes.

mappings.name: Program Download
mappings.primaryMitigation: Engineering access via PAM with session recording; OT monitoring detects all programming protocol activity; change management authorisation required; PLC program integrity check post-download.

mappings.name: Program Upload
mappings.primaryMitigation: OT monitoring detects all programming protocol activity including uploads; RBAC limits who has engineering access; PAM session recording.

mappings.name: Masquerading
mappings.primaryMitigation: Device authentication (SR-1.2) prevents protocol-level masquerading; OT monitoring with asset baseline detects new/impersonating devices; OT-aware DPI validates protocol behaviour.

mappings.name: Rootkit
mappings.primaryMitigation: Application whitelisting on OT workstations prevents rootkit installation; integrity monitoring detects persistence mechanisms; vendor-qualified patching closes rootkit installation vectors.

mappings.name: Scripting Interpreter
mappings.primaryMitigation: Application whitelisting restricts scripting interpreter execution; mobile code controls (SR-2.4); RBAC limits who can run scripts on OT systems.

mappings.name: Unauthorized Command Message
mappings.primaryMitigation: OT-aware firewall with DPI validates protocol commands and blocks unexpected function codes; passive monitoring detects anomalous command patterns; input validation for safety-critical commands.

mappings.name: Spoof Reporting Message
mappings.primaryMitigation: Communication integrity (SR-3.1) for critical data flows; OT monitoring provides independent network-level view of process state; tamper detection on field devices.

mappings.name: System Firmware
mappings.primaryMitigation: Verify firmware integrity before and after maintenance; restrict firmware update mode; vendor-qualified firmware only; tamper-evident seals detect physical firmware tampering.

mappings.name: Valid Accounts
mappings.primaryMitigation: Default credential elimination; MFA on all OT access; account lifecycle management; annual access reviews to remove stale accounts; anomalous login detection.

mappings.name: Wireless Compromise
mappings.primaryMitigation: WPA3 Enterprise authentication for OT WiFi; rogue AP detection; WirelessHART/ISA100 on isolated VLAN; spectrum monitoring; no wireless connectivity to safety systems.

mappings.name: Supply Chain Compromise
mappings.primaryMitigation: Vendor security assessment programme; SBOM requirement for new OT products; firmware integrity verification at installation; network isolation for newly deployed OT equipment during burn-in.

mappings.name: Spearphishing Attachment
mappings.primaryMitigation: Block email and internet access on OT workstations; application whitelisting prevents execution of phishing payloads; IDMZ email-scanning proxy if email is required for OT admin accounts.

mappings.name: Theft of Operational Information
mappings.primaryMitigation: RBAC restricts access to process data and engineering drawings; encryption for data in transit and on portable media; IDMZ prevents bulk data transfer from OT to IT without logging.

mappings.name: Remote System Discovery
mappings.primaryMitigation: Network segmentation limits discovery scope; passive OT monitoring detects active scanning (even low-intensity scanning is anomalous in well-baselined OT networks).

mappings.name: Modify Program
mappings.primaryMitigation: PLC program integrity checks (pre/post maintenance); OT monitoring detects programming protocol activity; SIS physical isolation is the primary control for safety system program modification.

mappings.name: Exploitation for Privilege Escalation
mappings.primaryMitigation: Timely patching of privilege escalation vulnerabilities; least-privilege RBAC reduces escalation value; application whitelisting prevents exploitation payloads.

mappings.name: Autorun Image
mappings.primaryMitigation: Disable autorun on all OT workstations and servers; USB scanning before introducing media; transient device management procedure; vendor-supplied media verified before use.
